note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location beyond west limb.
Measurement using source location just beyond the limb and SOHO imagery only due to STEREO A COR2 data gap.
Measurement of the bulk of the CME based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of the wider, fainter, SHOCK of the CME.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Two spacecraft measurement of the leading edge of the CME, capturing the half-width more closely in STEREO A than SOHO, where it appears wider.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO and STEREO Ahead; the CME could not be fit to the far-side.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Longitude based on the source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly as there is a data gap in SOHO imagery at the time of this event.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and the precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Parameters based on best fit of leading edge in SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 imagery using potential source location. The feature becomes too faint/diffused in later frames to track. No good STEREO A frames to use in measurement.

Two spacecraft measurement using triangulation of the leading edge to determine a potential longitude; uncertainty in longitude approximately +/- 15 degrees. Was found to likely not be associated with on-disk activity.
Best fit measurement between C2/C3 and COR2A. Leading edge accelerates in C2. STEREO A is in campaign mode for the Parker Solar Probe perihelion pass, affecting COR2A image cadence and quality.
Measurement based on SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and limited available frames of STEREO A COR2 imagery due to ongoing STEREO A campaign.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2, C3 and limited STEREO A COR2 frames, as the spacecraft was in a campaign. There appears to be some westward deflection.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement using limited STEREO A frames due to an ongoing campaign.
Measurement made using best fit between COR2A, abundant frames of C3, and a few frames of C2. STEREO A is currently in campaign mode for the Parker Solar Probe perihelion pass.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, confirmed by the source.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. Only one frame of STEREO A was used for this measurement due to the ongoing STEREO A campaign.
Very uncertain 1-coronagraph analysis based on the assumption that the source is the filament eruption north of the disk center.
Approximate analysis with two coronagraphs, with the fit also confirmed by the location of the source dimming. STEREO A coronagraph imagery is very sparse and low quality due to the campaign mode.
Analysis with two coronagraphs, with the fit confirmed by the source location (dimming).
Analysis of the second front of this CME (bulk?) based on the location of the source.

A very approximate analysis of the fainter earlier front of this CME based on the source location. Analysis is especially uncertain because of the faintness of the front in STEREO A coronagraph, the sparseness of the COR2A imagery b/c of the campaign mode.

Measurement made with SWPC_CAT using very limited SOHO imagery due to the faintness of the CME and only one frame of STEREO A COR2 due to its ongoing campaign. Measurements yield a ~10 degree range in longitudes from ~E20 to ~E30 and corresponding range of speeds all less than 375 km/s.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. STEREO A COR2 imagery is limited due to it ongoing campaign.
One-spacecraft measurement which considers that this eruption is associated with AR3835 on the Earth-facing disk, though this eruption may possibly be far-sided.

Updated measurement with a lower half-width to try and more closely match the observed type II shock speed for this event of ~1200 km/s. A very approximate analysis based on the fit of the complex-shaped faint shock with two coronagraphs.

Updated bulk measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery so only early imagery in SOHO LASCO C2 was used in this measurement. Multiple measurements produced speeds ranging from 487 to 632 km/s.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement made using source longitude as seen in UV imagery, as well as best fit using multiple SOHO LASCO C2/C3 frames and only one good frame of STEREO A COR2. There is a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery from 2024-10-02T14:08Z to 2024-10-02T17:08Z, in addition to being in campaign mode for the Parker Solar Probe perihelion pass which affects COR2A image quality and cadence.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 which is in campaign mode, but the CME was visible for several frames.
Measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. Due to the ongoing campaign, there were no useable STEREO A COR2 frames for this measurement.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
BULK measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2

SHOCK measurment triangulated between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. The shock front is notably wide with a "layered" appearance in both SOHO and STEREO with some apparent concentric fronts, along with being a bit "wider" than it was "taller" with an overall oval-shape in SOHO. The best fit between both shock fronts is presented here, though it does not capture 100% of the shock front which may be even wider.

Leading edge measurement using only SOHO C2/C3; the CME is not seen in early STEREO A frames, and then STEREO A data drops out, so this one-spacecraft measurement is approximate in that it has a fixed longitude; it is possible there is more westward deflection (higher longitude) and the latitude is lower.
Leading edge measurement using primarily SOHO C2/C3 and a few STEREO A frames; the measurement fit the observed source on the disk well.

SOHO leading edge measurement using a fixed, known longitude based on the source eruption in EUV imagery; the CME is not visibly seen in STEREO A imagery easily, especially during an ongoing STEREO A campaign, but it may potentially be seen to the southeast in STEREO A imagery.
Measurement of shock front using only SOHO imagery, since the CME is not visible well in STEREO A.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2

Parameters based on best fit of faint leading edge in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. There is a high level of uncertainty with this analysis due to multiple nearly simultaneous eruptions leading up to the CME in imagery. The width of the CME is very uncertain.
Approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. Parameters are uncertain because the CME front is not clear as it is overlapping with the preceding wider CME.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Few STEREO A frames were usable for this measurement due to the faintness of the CME.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. It incorporates the most faint western part of the halo in STEREO A COR2.

Very approximate analysis based only on the fit with two coronagraphs (since no source has been found in the EUV imagery). Latitude could be lower or higher (at least from 30 deg to 50 deg) but ~40 degrees seems to produce the best fit.
An uncertain measurement based on fit with two coronagraph and not confirmed by the source location (source not found).
Uncertain analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and not confirmed by source location (no source has been found).

A very approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs, not confirmed by the source location since the source is behind the limb. The longitude could be at least -10 to +10 degrees off.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. CME had been over taken by the time it entered SOHO LASCO C3. CME exhibits strong Southern deflection.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

An extremely approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, however the sole STEREO A image showing the front of this CME after the data gap is very poor quality, in addition to the source of this CME no having been found in EUV imagery.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

STEREOCat one-spacecraft (SOHO) bulk measurement with plane of sky angle set to -2 degrees in order to fix longitude; SWPC_Cat measurements of the leading edge of the CME best fit longitudes near 45 degrees west given the CMEs rounded appearance and presence in the south, which were felt to be unrealistic given the source location of the eruption.

Shock measurement using one spacecraft (SOHO) C2 and C3 images which fits the widest extent of the south and southwestern portions of the CME leading edge as well as a north-northeastern portion that reveals itself in later SOHO frames after 22:30Z which is assumed to be part of the CME shock, though it is disjoint. This measurement is in support of solar energetic particle modeling activities, and as such, is not an approximation of the source location and should not be used for trajectory analysis.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is limited imagery for this event and various CME fronts/outflow features that make it difficult to track the leading edge in coronagraph imagery. Because of this the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. There is uncertainty in this measurement due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery and limited imagery in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The measured parameters may therefore vary slightly.

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and an early frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT, as well as candidate source location beyond the limb. This measurement is approximate, and the longitude may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees.
Very approximate measurement based on the fit in two coronagraphs and confirmed by the possible location of the source (dimming). Only a few timestamps are available for this front in coronagraphs.

Two-coronagraph analysis of the complex-shaped (two-lobed) brighter front we consider to be the bulk of this CME. This analysis is based on the location of the X flare as the source, which is confirmed by a good fit with two coronagraphs. This analysis encompasses both the more eastern lobe/front of this CME and the more western lobe, which are best seen in LASCO C2.
SHOCK measurement made using running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Two-coronagraph analysis of the western loop-like front/lobe of the complex halo CME complex. Analysis is based on the source location (approximate center of the erupting filament) and confirmed by a decent fit in two coronagraphs. Encompasses only the western lobe/front of this complex (with the more western lobe deemed to be the X-flare associated separate CME).

Measurement using approximate longitude of source location observed in UV imagery along with the best fit in available frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 imagery. The leading edge of this CME was not captured by COR2A due to a data gap.

Best fit measurement using several frames of C3 and COR2A. This CME was affected by a data gap in STEREO A COR2 lasting from 2024-10-10T05:08Z to 2024-10-10T08:53Z. Image quality for this CME was also affected by the ongoing comet campaign [and calibrations?]
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery and one frame of STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters may vary slightly as the source is likely far-sided and there is no source location to assist in the measurement.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is likely far-sided and there is no precise source location for this event, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured longitude may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and there is no precise source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Triangulated measurement between STEREO COR2A and SOHO LASCO C2/C3, notably difficult due to faintness and diffuse nature of the front in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 compared to the more visible front in STEREO COR2A. Far-sided longitudes did not fit at all between coronagraphs, so despite the lack of clear on-disk source, this CME appears front sided and this best fit is provided.

Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Using later frames to capture a hopefully more accurate speed as the CME speeds up in later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery of the bulk of the CME. The fainter potential shock portion to the SW is excluded from this analysis.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Event is very faint in COR2A
Parameters based on best fit of most defined leading edge. There is material ahead of the measured front that was not included in this CME measurement.
Measurement using source location and SOHO imagery. No usable STEREO A COR2 imagery for measurement.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location.
Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on the source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. Limited imagery was available for this measurement due to data gaps in real-time in SOHO and STEREO A coronagraph imagery.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3. There was no SOHO LASCO C2 or STEREO A COR2 imagery available for this measurement, as the leading edge had already left the field of view when imagery returned from the data gaps.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2

Measurement of the bulk feature based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Very approximate analysis based on a fit of the front in two coronagraphs, however there are multiple consecutive fronts seen in coronagraph. The source (very faint opening of field lines behind the limb in the southwest) indicates a source far behind the limb. There is a range of longitudes that gives a good fit with two coronagraphs.
Approximate analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit in two coronagraph and confirmed by the location of the source (the larger, central, part of the filament eruption).
Updated measurement to a few more solar radii.
A very approximate measurement based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The source is likely behind the limb and the fit gives a range of possible longitudes. Additionally, the front is very fuzzy.

These parameters are based on the source location of the M6.5 flare. The CME features are not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery. Due to single spacecraft mode, there is a larger amount of uncertainty with this measurement.
Parameters based on best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs. Only backsided longitude and high southern latitude produce a fit.
Approximate analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs. The source is behind the limb and the front is faint and diffuse. Not simulated because there are no missions in the CME's path.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement to approximate longitude; longitude parameter has uncertainty of about +/- 15 degrees.

Two spacecraft leading edge measurement (SOHO, STEREO A) to triangulate the longitude; the leading edge was found to be consistent with a far-sided eruption rather than the source on the Earth-facing disk.
Bulk measurement of the brightest portion associated with the CME using SOHO C2, C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation to determine longitude; uncertainty in longitude is up to +/- 20 or 25 degrees.
Bulk measurement taking into account the brightest portion of the CME using only SOHO and assuming the source is similar in longitude to the previous eruption to which it is sympathetic.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Because the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Parameters based on best fit of CME in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement triangulated betweeen SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery.

Triangulated measurement using SDO observations and between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO COR2A coronagraphs, using some frames of SOHO LASCO C3 with notable trouble following the front as the leading edge travels directly into the LASCO C3 pylon.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, focusing on the brighter distinct nose more in the ecliptic over the more diffuse, dimmer material traveling towards the south in the coronagraphs, which proved difficult to capture in one measurement between the craft and may be outflow from this broad eruption as seen in SDO AIA imagery.
Best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraph imagery. Multiple frames of C3 and COR2A difference imagery were available to make the measurement.
These parameters are based on the best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. SOHO LASCO C3 was not usable due to being behind the control arm in C3's view.
Updated measurement with additional SOHO C2 and C3 frames that were not previously available due to a data gap.
Bulk measurement of the CME using SOHO and STEREO A coronagraphs. Uncertainty in longitude of up to 20 degrees.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO/STEREO A to triangulate a potential source longitude; possible uncertainty in longitude up to 15 degrees.
BULK measurement based on source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

SHOCK MEASUREMENT triangulated between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A, following the outermost leading edge of the wider, fainter shock traveling ahead of the bulk. This notably does not capture the entire shock-like feature observed in both SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs in the SWPC_CAT measurement tool. This fit follows the leading edge of the shock best, cutting short from the widest portions of the partial halo.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Event was blocked by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. C3 was not available for measurement due to event being blocked by the pylon.
Measurement based on approximate source location. There was no available STEREO A COR2 imagery available for measurement and this event was also blocked by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location. There is no STEREO A COR2 imagery available for measurement due to data gap.
Measurement based on approximate source location. There is no STEREO A COR2 imagery available for this measurement due to a data gap.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT, as well as potential source location.
While SOHO white light imagery is not available for this event, running difference imagery is available for SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to limited image availability in STEREO A COR2.
Parameters based on the best fit in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Mostly SOHO LASCO C2 frames used due to faintness of event in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery and limited STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 using potential source location. No STEREO A COR2 imagery available due to data gap.

Measurement of the bulk portion based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is wide and difficult to fit between coronagraphs, with measured longitudes ranging from -41 to -66.

SHOCK MEASUREMENT based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery tracking the wider, faint front which precedes the bulk of this CME. This measurement does not capture the entire shock feature observed in both coronagraphs, and instead follows the leading edge of the shock best, missing the widest portions of the partial halo.
Leading edge measurement using two spacecraft showing southward deflection of the eruption.
Triangulation accomplished using two spacecraft leading edge measurement.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Limited STEREO A frames available for this event.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/STEREO COR2A, and the observed source just beyond the SW limb in SDO AIA imagery. SOHO LASCO is currently in a comet observation campaign affecting imagery, notably LASCO C3 running difference which does not observe the front at all while it can be seen in C3 white light. Enough C2 and COR2A imagery is available and usable for a confident fit, nonetheless.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Triangulation based on the SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 white light imagery in StereoCAT.
StereoCAT measurement based on triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3 white light imagery.
Measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 white light imagery in StereoCAT.

BULK MEASUREMENT based on the approximate source location. This measurement relies on only a few frames of SOHO LASCO C3 imagery, for which the CME is near the end of the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view. Multiple measurements resulted in longitudes spanning 41 to 54 degrees and speeds ranging from 800 to 1054 km/s. This measurement is speculative due to the limited image availability at the time of this event.

SHOCK measurement based on the approximate source location tracking the faint, wider feature surrounding the bulk portion of this CME. This measurement is preliminary due to limited coronagraph imagery and the measured parameters may vary.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 using SWPC_CAT.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 using SWPC_CAT.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 using SWPC_CAT.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 using SWPC_CAT.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 using SWPC_CAT.
Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2, the measured parameters could not be confirmed using triangulation and may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement of faint leading edge using best fit between C2 and COR2A coronagraphs. Leading edge becomes diffuse before reaching C3 field of view.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement of the leading edge using best fit between coronagraphs and location of source observed in UV imagery. Data gap in SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 from 2024-10-31T11:18Z to 2024-10-31T13:30Z, but this did not severely inhibit this measurement since the leading edge remains in the field of view for several other frames.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Only one frame of this CME was visible in SOHO LASCO C2 and it was not measurable in SOHO LASCO C3. STEREO A COR2 imagery was primarily used for this measurement.
Measurement based on the approximate source location using STEREO A COR2 imagery only.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement based on the best fit between one frame of available STEREO A COR2 imagery and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT. SOHO LASCO C2 imagery was used for the majority of this measurement due to limited imagery availability.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement of the bulk of the CME based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT
Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and limited STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

Triangulated measurement using STEREO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, with notable difficulty using COR2A running difference imagery with only two available frames over the duration of this CME due to a data gap. The on-disk source flares observed in SDO AIA 131 also assisted in triangulating this event to this source, ruling out far-sided fits.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.
This measurement is more approximate than usual due to a large data gap in SOHO LASCO C3, and is based on limited available STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Leading edge measurement using STEREO A and SOHO C2 occurring after a data backfill.
Updated 3D measurement with backfilled SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery
Measurement based on the best fit of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
BULK measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.

SHOCK measurement following the faint feature surrounding the bright bulk portion. The outermost, widest feature of the shock is not included in this measurement. Measured parameters based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2/STEREO A.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Event was too faint to be reliably tracked in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the location of the source (the erupting filament) also confirmed by the fit of the front in two coronagraphs.
Approximate measurement based on the best fit of the front in two coronagraph and is in accordance with the location of the source.
Measurement triangulated between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO COR2A imagery, and based on the approximate source location which is visible slightly beyond the northwest limb.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2 based on on-disk source location.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. This CME front is notably more visible in later frames of running difference imagery despite its faintness in white light imagery.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. This CME front is notably more visible in later frames of running difference imagery despite its faintness in white light imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. CME occurs during data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement of faint leading edge using source location and best fit in C2/C3. Not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap lasting from 2024-11-12T13:23Z to 2024-11-12T23:53Z.
Measurement using best fit between C3 and COR2A and a far-sided source location as indicated by UV imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location as there is no STEREO A COR2 imagery available for this event.

Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and one frame of STEREO A COR2. The STEREO A COR 2 frame is fairly far out in the field of view, so while it confirms a far sided measurement, this event may fit with longitudes between -120 and -140.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Possible range in location from E115 to E125 and N35 to N40.

Measured longitude based on the source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly as this event is not measurable in STEREO A COR2 and therefore cannot be confirmed using stereoscopic viewpoints.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

The precise source location is unknown as the source is visible beyond the eastern limb. However, the measured longitude was based on an approximate source location of -100 degrees. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result of this approximation.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2A. This event was too faint to be reliably tracked in SOHO LASCO C3
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
One-spacecraft measurement (SOHO) of the CME leading edge out to about 7 solar radii assuming some southern deflection and constraining the longitude.
Updated measurement based on additional available coronagraph imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location beyond limb.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location beyond the west limb.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2A
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location on Earth-facing disk and running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3.

Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, there is no stereoscopic viewpoint to confirm the measured parameters which may vary slightly as a result.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement using triangulation to fit the source longitude.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation to determine the source longitude.

Measurement using an abundance of only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 frames, using a true longitude of W90 based on the source location seen in UV imagery. STEREO A COR2 was in a data gap lasting from 2024-11-19T12:38Z to 2024-11-20T00:23Z.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measured longitude based on the approximate source location, which is visible near the western limb.

Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, the measured parameters could not be confirmed using stereoscopic viewpoints and they may vary slightly as a result.

Analysis based solely on the best fit with two coronagraphs. The erupting filament seems to be much more central that the longitude in this analysis, so there must have been some deflection - or the source was more eastern.
Imperfect analysis of the bulk front based on the fit in two coronagraphs (note that only two timestamps for COR2A are available at the time of analysis).

Very uncertain analysis based on the fit with ONE coronagraph only (SOHO) since there is no imagery from STEREO A COR2 because of an extensive data gap and the source is backsided, based on the coronal signatures. The range of possible longitudes that produce a decent fit in SOHO is from 115 to 140 degrees.
Preliminary analysis based on the SHOCK of the CME. Higher degree of uncertainty with parameters due to single-spacecraft analysis.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. CME appears to speed up in later frames. Earlier frames measured speeds around 300-350km/s.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, finding a good far-sided fit between the coronagraphs.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This event is not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 running difference imagery as it is obscured by the pylon.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A, finding a good far-sided, high southern latitude fit between the coronagraphs.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This event is not measurable in SOHO LASCO C3 due to faint leading edge.

Parameters based on potential source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. The CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 difference imagery due to the pylon blocking most of the CME. It is also not visible in STEREO A COR2 difference imagery due to a data gap.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location. The leading edge of the CME fades away quickly in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3 difference imagery.
Measurement based on the source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as source location beyond the limb.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Limited imagery was available for this measurement as the CME fades away quickly in later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. The CME is obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location. The CME was blocked by the pylon and not measurable in SOHO LASCO C3.
Parameters based on potential source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

As the source is visible slightly beyond the western limb, a precise source location is unknown. However, the source is visible near the western limb so a measured longitude of 90 degrees was used. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result of the unknown/far-sided source location. This measurement tracks the brighter leading edge portion and excludes the faint feature preceding this.

Parameters are based on best fit of feature in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery with guidance of directionality of features seen after a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery. Since the features aren't clear in STEREO A COR2 imagery it was not usable for this fit. Due to this, there is a higher degree of uncertainty with this CME analysis.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 and probable source location.
Parameters based on best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery using potential source location for longitude.

Measurement based on source location and fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 frames only. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2, likely due to the faintness of CME as seen from STEREO A's perspective. (This CME is oriented farther out of STEREO-A's plane of sky than SOHO LASCO's plane of sky, making it less likely to be visible in STEREO-A than in SOHO.)

Measurement of faint leading edge using best fit between C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, as well as observation of field line movement that may be an eruptive signature beyond the E limb, as seen in GOES SUVI imagery of the Earth-facing disk.

Approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs, the latitude of the analysis seems to be more southern compared to the latitude of the possible source, so there was possibly some southward deflection.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. Only one timestamp is available in STEREO A COR2 because of a data gap.
Approximate measurement of the well showing flux rope (not the fuzzier front) based on fitting with two coronagraphs, confirmed by the possible source (small eruption near limb).
Approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs.
Two spacecraft leading edge measurement encompassing all but the eastern most portion of the CME leading edge.
Leading edge/bulk measurement of the CME using SOHO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
One spacecraft leading edge measurement approximating the source longitude based on EUV imagery.
Measured longitude based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Very approximate analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Two-spacecraft bulk measurement of the CME taking into account the brightest portions of the CME bulk as the leading edge became untraceable about midway into the CME propagation through LASCO coronagraph imagery.

Initial plane-of-sky measurement made with running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 using SWPC_CAT. Measurement was updated to have a 3D longitude of E90, as source seems to be on/along the East limb, and fits between E85 and E105 were achieved.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on approximate location of source as observed in UV imagery and based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 frames. This CME was not captured in STEREO A COR2 due to overnight data gap which lasted from 2024-12-03T12:23Z to 2024-12-04T00:38Z.

Measurement based on approximate location of source as observed in UV imagery and based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 frames. This CME was not captured in STEREO A COR2 due to overnight data gap which lasted from 2024-12-03T12:23Z to 2024-12-04T00:38Z.

Measurement of faint leading edge using C2 and COR2A. This CME was not visible in C3 difference imagery; it was nearly entirely blocked by the pylon and the partial edge that could be seen was extremely faint and unreliable to track. The leading edge is faint and quickly becomes diffuse in both C2 and COR2A.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Two spacecraft measurement using triangulation to determine longitude.
Bulk measurement as the leading edge becomes diffuse with time, utilizing both SOHO and STEREO A for triangulation.

Preliminary leading edge measurement using one spacecraft (SOHO) which considers a large latitude to fit visual observations; uncertainty in longitude is quite large and may be as large as +/- 30 degrees.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO and STEREO A difference imagery.
Parameters based on best fit of CME in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
These parameters are based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO and STEREO A coronagraph imagery.
Parameters based on the best fit with two coronagraphs.
Parameters based on the best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 difference imagery.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The front in STEREO A COR2 is super faint and fuzzy.
Parameters based on the best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Best fit measurement using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2; fits very well to source location (~S10W50) observed in UV imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. The CME is partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge becomes too diffuse to measure after a few frames, and the measured parameters rely on early imagery as a result.

Analysis based on the best fit with 2 coronagraphs, somewhat contradicting the location of the flare (long ~-77 deg). Analysis is complicated by a large data gap in SOHO, as well as by the partial obscuring of this CME by SOHO C3 pylon. Longitude of the CME could be as high as -70 degrees (with a different fit with 2 coronagraphs in the tool)
Analysis based on the best fit in two coronagraphs and confirmed by the location of the associated flare.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by location of the flare.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs and later confirmed by the possible source (continuous activity all morning culminating with 08Z dimming in AR 3920).
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. It differs a bit from the location of the associated flare, so there is some westward deflection, in addition to significant southward deflection.

Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and one frame in STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is high uncertainty due to lack of clear source and having only one frame in STEREO A COR2 imagery of this CME.

This analysis is based off of the best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. The front becomes too diffuse/faint to track before it exits the C2 field of view. It is not clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C3 difference imagery.

These parameters are based on the potential source location (accounting for deflection of source to the NE) and the best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 difference imagery. The front has a diffuse front making for tricky analysis.

These parameters are based on the potential source location (accounting for deflection of source to the NE) and the best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 difference imagery. The front is pretty diffuse in SOHO LASCO C2 difference imagery but becomes slightly more clear in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery, however there are overlapping features making this analysis a bit uncertain.
This CME deflects far to the south with continued deflection southward as it progresses through the SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 field of view. Due to this, there is some uncertainty with the fit.
Parameters based off of the best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and C3 using the potential source location for longitude and adjusting the latitude to fit the CME.
Based on the fit with two coronagraphs, which confirms the source location as the location of the likely associated Active Region 3920 which is showing some minor activity.

Two spacecraft leading edge measurement using triangulation with difficulty seeing the CME leading edge in SOHO LASCO C2 due to an obscuring CME in front of the CME in question; GONG data supports the eruption coming from a far-sided source with this approximate longitude.
Analysis based on the best fit in two coronagraphs but confirmed by source location (taking into account southward deflection).
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, confirmed by the location of the associated flare.
One-spacecraft measurement attempting to fit both the south/south-southeast bulk measurement as well as the northeast portion in the SOHO LASCO C2 coronagraph.
Shock front measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 incorporating the southeastward leading edge and western/eastern leading edges simultaneously in one circular-shaped measurement.
Bulk measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 mainly focusing on brighter south portion of the CME.
Two spacecraft bulk measurement capturing the main shape of the CME inside of the protruding feature (i.e. the CME could be faster than this measurement).
One spacecraft measurement of the leading edge, holding the known longitude constant.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and limited STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Reanalysis of this event in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A going more northern and less wide, noting difficulty in tracking the same faint front between both coronagraphs on top of limited STEREO COR2A availaibility.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as well as source location in SWPC_CAT. Only one frame of STEREO A COR2 was available for a measurement, and the majority of the measurement uses SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Preliminary one spacecraft measurement assuming that the eruption originates from the far-side.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO COR2A. CME starts smaller than a 10 HW, expanding wider further into the FOV, and is notably deflected southward from the source region as corroborated in GOES SUVI.
Analysis with one coronagraph (SOHO) based on the location of the source (ejecta from former Active Region 3916).

Very uncertain analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and giving preference to the minor eruption in AR 3916 as the source. However, the fit with the longitude of AR 3916 doesn't work, so there seems to be a deflection.

A very approximate/uncertain analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2) and since the longitude of the source eruption does not produce a good fit. The range of possible longitudes and latitudes is very high, with longitudes ranging from 30 to 50 deg.
Analysis  based on the best fit in two coronagraphs.
Approximate analysis based on a fit with two coronagraphs, but the front is fuzzy especially in STEREO A.
Approximate analysis based on the fit in two coronagraphs and on the location of the source (erupting filament).
Analysis based on the best fit in two coronagraphs and confirmed by one possible source (in STEREO EUV imagery).
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery and potential source location.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2. Measurements with similar parameters using possible front seen in SOHO LASCO C3 were also made, but with lower confidence.
Parameters based on best fit of features in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME has a messy front so some uncertainty may be present with parameters.
BULK measurement based on probable far sided source and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
SHOCK measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and one frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery.
One spacecraft leading edge measurement using an approximate longitude as determined from EUV imagery; there is probably a 10 to 15 degree uncertainty in longitude.
One spacecraft measurement based on visual inspection of source location in EUV imagery.
Measurement based on source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Measurement based on the best fit with SOHO LASCO C3 imagery in SWPC_CAT. No STEREO A COR2 imagery was available for a measurement. This measurement primarily tracks the fainter shock feature to the south of the halo, but the leading edge of the bulk is traveling with the shock and the leading edge is indistinguishable from the shock.

Measurement based on best fit between C2/C3 and COR2A, as well as source location seen in EUV imagery. Part of this CME is not captured in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2024-12-18T11:38Z to 2024-12-18T16:36Z.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery.

Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is some uncertainty about the measurement and triangulation of this CME because it is very faint in STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on potential source location just beyond the east limb as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of the leading edge with an upper bound on half-width.
Low confidence measurement using two spacecraft triangulation to determine the source longitude.
Measurement based on source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Analysis based on the best fit with two coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs.
Measurement based on source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.
